name: WMI Event Subscriptions (Registry)
categoryDescription: Registry keys that cause code to execute at boot, logon, or via Windows subsystem hooks.
description: Registry-side WMI Event Subscription data — the WBEM\ESS key reflects active permanent WMI subscriptions (EventFilter, EventConsumer, FilterToConsumerBinding).
forensic_value: WMI subscriptions are one of the most persistent and detection-resistant mechanisms available. The registry key shows subscription names but the full definition is in the WMI repository (Objects.data). Cross-reference with WMI-Activity Operational log EID 5861.
